text: Handling deadlines is a key skill in both work and study. Good time management involves breaking large tasks into smaller steps and setting personal deadlines before the official one. Prioritization helps you decide which tasks are most urgent or important, so you can focus your energy where it matters most. Scope management means knowing when to say "no" to extra work that could delay your progress. Finally, communication is essential; if you sense you might miss a deadline, it’s better to inform your team or manager early and propose a realistic solution. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and meet deadlines consistently.
